title: SQL Server Connect error in current nighly build
author: JotunJosh
body_snippet:
In the current nightly build i cannot connect to my seperate sql server. On the other side my stable client has no problems to connect to the same sql server (other database)

> 2025-04-03 08:32:39,754 [1] ERROR - Retrieving database version failed. System.TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for 'Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.TdsParser' threw an exception.
 ---> System.TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for 'Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SNILoadHandle' threw an exception.
 ---...

iterations.description: Issue lacks reproducible steps and expected vs actual behavior on current build; the x64/x32 BadImageFormatException was noted as fixed by the reporter, but no confirmation the issue is fully resolved or which library/version is affected.

comments.author: JotunJosh
comments.snippet:
Here are some information about the sql server in the background if that help:
Microsoft SQL Server Express (64-bit)
Version: 15.0.2000.5

Both DB's working and not working one

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: Thanks, i am looking into this issue, something wrong with a lib, its build for x64, but actualy try to use x32 and crashing with: BadImageFormatException: An attempt was made to load a program with an incorrect format. (0x8007000B)

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: little update, I manage to fix lib issue, but once I am there - will update UI for DB integration and script works as well

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `3670bffb` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** SQL Server Connect error in current nighly build

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

notes:
AI triage (claude): Issue lacks reproducible steps and expected vs actual behavior on current build; the x64/x32 BadImageFormatException was noted as fixed by the reporter, but no confirmation the issue is fully resolved or which library/version is affected.
Approach: Request confirmation of current status on latest fork build with exact steps to reproduce, error messages, and which SQL Server library version is being used
